summary: A small toolkit to normalize Ember-style composition CSVs, fit VAR(1) models in CLR space, compute block-mean covariance traces, calibrate theoretical matrix bounds for Shannon entropy changes, and produce calibrated JSON summaries and bootstrap empirical bounds.
purpose: Provide reproducible calibration and diagnostics for theoretical bounds on entropy change (H) across multiple time scales; enable automated batch runs, PCA fallback for stability, and empirical bootstrap checks.

scripts.purpose: Original calibrator (robustified to accept BOMs, drop tiny carriers, ridge + shrink to enforce stationarity).
scripts.notes: Reads *_eitt_summary.json files in outputs and writes *_calibrated.json next to each summary.

scripts.purpose: Standalone diagnostics: normalize CSV, fit VAR(1), print eigenvalues, spectral radius, trace(Sigma_u), trace(Gamma0), and block traces for requested M values.
PCA→VAR calibrator: fit PCA on CLR rows, fit VAR(1) on top-k PC scores, compute block traces in PC space and map back to CLR trace approximation.
Tuned calibrator with PCA fallback and CLI flags (--reg_lambda, --pca-k). Use this for robust batch runs.
scripts.notes: Will fall back to PCA→VAR when direct VAR is unstable.

scripts.purpose: Bootstrap empirical bounds for percent change in Shannon entropy using non-overlapping block resampling.

excerpt_sentences: For the annual ladder level (window_days = 365) the calibrated JSON reports "block_cov_trace": 30.763391122100337.
The same calibrated JSON reports "refined_calibrated_bound_pct": 363.4326334767325 for the annual level.
PCA k=2 calibration produced an annual block trace of "block_cov_trace": 10.54219868570116.
The PCA k=2 refined bound for the annual level is "refined_calibrated_bound_pct": 124.54345542637103.

installation.python: 3.10+ recommended (your project used Python 3.13 in the session).
installation.venv: Create and activate a virtual environment; install dependencies.
pip_commands: pip install numpy scipy pandas scikit-learn
installation.notes: scikit-learn is required for PCA scripts; tuned calibrator will fall back to non-PCA logic if scikit-learn is absent but PCA features will be disabled.

features.robust_json_read: All calibrators read summary JSON with encoding='utf-8-sig' to tolerate BOMs.
features.normalization: Heuristic detection of time/carrier/value columns; pivot to time x parts and closure to compositions; drops tiny carriers (mean <= 1e-8) to avoid numerical instability.
features.clr_transform: Safe log with small EPS floor and centering to produce CLR rows.
features.var1_fit: Ridge-regularized OLS for VAR(1) with sample covariance for residuals; optional reg_lambda CLI flag.
features.stationarity_enforcement: If spectral radius >= 1, calibrators shrink A by factor 0.99/rho to enforce stationarity.
features.pca_fallback: When direct VAR is unstable, PCA→VAR is used (configurable k) to stabilize estimation and reduce dimensionality.
features.block_cov_trace: Computes block-mean covariance trace for window sizes M and reports block_cov_trace in calibrated JSON.
features.refined_bound: Computes refined theoretical fraction and reports refined_calibrated_bound_pct (percent) per ladder level.
features.bootstrap: Empirical bootstrap of block-mean percent changes for practical uncertainty quantification.

commands_reference.run_all_batch: python -m src.batch_run_eitt && python -m src.ar1_calibrate --outputs outputs --data_dir "data\ember data"

key_checks: Spectral radius of A (max |eig(A)|) must be < 1 for Lyapunov solver to be valid.
trace(Sigma_u) should be positive and of reasonable magnitude relative to H_star.
block_cov_trace should be positive; negative or NaN indicates non-stationarity or solver failure.
Compare refined_calibrated_bound_pct to observed pct_change_H: orders-of-magnitude gaps indicate overly conservative bound or scaling issues.

good: Pipeline robustified to tolerate UTF-8 BOMs and to drop tiny carriers that destabilize CLR transforms.
Ridge regularization plus shrink-to-stationary prevents Lyapunov solver failures and produces finite block traces.
PCA→VAR fallback yields much smaller and more stable block traces for datasets with near-unit eigenvalues.
Bootstrap script provides empirical uncertainty to complement conservative theoretical bounds.
bad: For the Europe monthly dataset, direct VAR(1) fit produced an eigenvalue > 1 (spectral radius ≈ 1.0185), causing Lyapunov instability and nonsensical block traces until shrink/PCA were applied.
Even after tuning and PCA, theoretical refined bounds remain conservative (often hundreds of percent) and can be orders of magnitude larger than observed pct_change_H at short/medium scales.
Some PCA runs encountered edge cases when k equals the data dimension or when residual matrices become 1-D; code now guards but users should choose k <= min(T-1, D).

proposed_solutions.solution: Enforce stationarity by shrinking A (A <- A * 0.99 / rho) and/or increase ridge regularization (reg_lambda up to 1e-4). If instability persists, use PCA→VAR with k small (1–3).

Report both theoretical bounds and empirical bootstrap percentiles. Consider fitting VAR on a lower-dimensional subspace (PCA) or on first differences of CLR if unit roots persist; investigate scaling of H_star and lambda_max(W).
Drop carriers with mean <= 1e-8 (configurable). Optionally aggregate rare carriers into an 'other' category before closure.
Document scikit-learn as a dependency and include 'pip install scikit-learn' in install steps; tuned calibrator will still run without PCA but will fall back automatically.

integration_notes_for_claude_coworker.goal: Copy the entire folder into Claude coworker workspace and continue development (tuning, adding tests, CI).
suggested_tasks: Add unit tests for normalize_and_pivot with synthetic CSVs covering edge cases (missing date, many tiny carriers, single carrier).
Add CI step to run diagnostics on a small sample dataset and assert spectral radius < 1 after tuning or that PCA fallback was used.
Add a README.md at repo root documenting commands, environment setup, and recommended defaults (reg_lambda, pca_k, tiny threshold).
Add a small example Jupyter notebook that runs diagnostics, PCA sweep (k=1..4), and plots refined bounds vs observed pct_change_H.

next_steps_recommendation: 1) Paste this folder into Claude coworker workspace.
2) Run the tuned calibrator across all summaries with: python -m src.ar1_calibrate_tuned --outputs outputs --data_dir "data\ember data" --reg_lambda 1e-6 --pca-k 3
3) Run bootstrap for datasets where theoretical bounds are large: python -m src.eitt_bootstrap_bounds --raw <csv> --Ms 1,7,30,91,365 --B 2000 --out outputs/<dataset>_bootstrap.json
4) Add unit tests and a README; iterate on reg_lambda and pca_k defaults based on batch diagnostics.
contact: If you want, I can also produce a single PowerShell wrapper (run_all.ps1) that runs normalization, calibrator, PCA sweep, and bootstrap for every CSV in data\ember data and writes a master CSV summary of block_cov_trace and refined bounds.
